warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Method–provenance block (reproducibility essentials)

Property: atom-resolved Fukui functions ( f⁺, f⁻, f⁰ ) and dual descriptor; headline figure reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index, f⁺ = 0.22885456 on the Fe centre  
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) as implemented in xtb 6.x  
Geometry provenance: user-supplied ferrocene.xyz (21 atoms) taken as-is; no re-optimization – single-point charge analyses only (notation: Fukui//GFN2-xTB)  
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)  
Total charge / multiplicity: 0 / 1 (closed-shell singlet for N, doublets for N ± 1 states generated internally by the skill)  
Software: xtb (GFN2-xTB); wrapper – chemkit fukui-reactivity skill  
Convergence & caveats: xtb SCC cycles converged; integrity check reports three WARNINGS (all echoed verbatim above). No SCF instabilities detected, and both Σf⁺ and Σf⁻ equal 1.0000, so charge conservation holds.  
Live log: the run streamed to a file announced at launch (e.g. `fukui_20260702-221852.out`) – it can be inspected or `tail -f`’d for full SCC and population details. JSON result saved as `ferrocene_fukui.json`.

Headline result

• Most electrophilic site: atom 11 (Fe) with f⁺ = 0.22885456  
• Most nucleophilic site: atom 11 (Fe) with f⁻ = 0.25938191  

The value reported above (0.22885456) is copied verbatim from the tool output and represents the maximum f⁺ Fukui index.
